audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Jethro, Zipporah and sons, testimony of deliverance, meal before Divine, delegation of judges, and sustainable leadership.

verses.restored: Jethro, priest of Midian, father-in-law of Moses, heard all that Divine had done for Moses and for Israel his people, that YHWH brought Israel out from Egypt.
Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, took Zipporah, wife of Moses, after her sending away,
and her two sons, one whose name was Gershom, for he said, I have been a sojourner in foreign land,
and name of the other was Eliezer, for Divine of my father was my help and rescued me from sword of Pharaoh.
Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, and his sons and his wife came to Moses, to wilderness where he was camping at mountain of Divine.
He said to Moses, I, your father-in-law Jethro, am coming to you, and your wife and her two sons with her.
Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, bowed, and kissed him; each asked his companion of peace, and they came into the tent.
Moses recounted to his father-in-law all that YHWH had done to Pharaoh and Egypt on account of Israel, all hardship that had found them on the way, and how YHWH rescued them.
Jethro rejoiced over all the good that YHWH had done to Israel, whom he rescued from hand of Egypt.
Jethro said, Blessed be YHWH, who rescued you from hand of Egypt and from hand of Pharaoh, who rescued the people from under hand of Egypt.
Now I know that YHWH is greater than all gods, because in the matter in which they acted arrogantly against them.
Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, took burnt offering and sacrifices for Divine; Aaron came and all elders of Israel to eat bread with Moses' father-in-law before the Divine.
It happened next day Moses sat to judge the people, and people stood around Moses from morning until evening.

Moses' father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people and said, What is this thing you are doing for the people? Why do you sit alone, and all the people stand around you from morning until evening?
Moses said to his father-in-law, Because the people come to me to inquire of Divine.
When they have a matter, it comes to me, and I judge between a man and his companion, and I make known statutes of the Divine and his Torah/instructions.
Moses' father-in-law said to him, The thing you are doing is not good.
Fading, you will fade away, both you and this people with you, for the matter is too heavy for you; you are not able to do it alone.
Now listen to my voice; I will counsel you, and may Divine be with you. You be for the people before the Divine, and bring the matters to the Divine.
You shall warn them with statutes and Torah/instructions, and make known to them the way they shall walk and the work they shall do.
But you shall see from all the people capable men, fearing/revering Divine, men of truth, hating unjust gain, and set them over them as chiefs of thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens.
They shall judge the people at every time; every great matter they shall bring to you, and every small matter they shall judge themselves. Make it lighter from upon you, and they shall bear with you.
If you do this thing, and Divine commands you, then you will be able to stand, and also all this people will go to their place in peace.
Moses listened to voice of his father-in-law and did all that he said.
Moses chose capable men from all Israel and gave them as heads over the people, chiefs of thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens.
They judged the people at every time; hard matter they brought to Moses, and every small matter they judged themselves.
Moses sent his father-in-law away, and he went to his land.

translation_notes: Jethro is Midianite priest and gives true wisdom; preserve non-Israelite wisdom and hospitality.
Leadership sustainability is explicit: 'not good,' too heavy, you cannot do it alone.
Delegation requires capable, truth-grounded people who hate unjust gain.
Zipporah and sons re-enter the narrative; do not erase them.
safety_note: Exodus 18 supports distributed leadership, not charismatic bottlenecking. It is a major anti-burnout and anti-domination text.
